heroHeadline: El Mayo Defense Memo Due Today; Sentencing Set April 13 — 614 Days of Cartel Civil War
heroSubtitle: 614 days of intra-cartel war since Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada's alleged kidnapping — 2,400+ homicides, 9,000+ families displaced. La Mayiza controls approximately 90% of former Chapitos territory. El Mayo's defense sentencing memo is due today (March 30); sentencing before Judge Brian Cogan on April 13, 2026 with mandatory life imprisonment and $15 billion forfeiture. Chapitos security chief 'El Chuta' indicted in Chicago on narcoterrorism charges. A Sinaloa affiliate sentenced to 15+ years in Georgia on March 27, illustrating the cartel's continued US distribution reach despite internal fragmentation.
footerNote: Data sourced from Noroeste, Riodoce, Proceso, InSight Crime, Reuters, AP, Animal Político, ACLED, DEA, SEDENA, SEMAR, Al Jazeera, AFP, Infobae
